data.description:
Title: Review wl-audit scheduler command (AM-0MNFSNJ3W00787T5)

Problem statement
The wl-audit scheduled command in AMPA appears to be non-functional and needs a focused code review to identify bugs, missing behaviors, and to produce a concise pseudo-code representation to guide fixes or reimplementation.

Users
- Operators / maintainers of AMPA who rely on scheduled audits to assess project health.
- Automation engineers who will implement or test fixes.

User stories
- As an operator, I want the wl-audit scheduled command to run reliably so audits are produced on schedule.
- As a maintainer, I want a clear pseudo-code representation and a list of defects so I can implement fixes with confidence.

Success criteria
1. A thorough code review is produced with a pseudo-code summary of the current implementation.
2. All correctness, reliability, and scheduling issues are identified and documented with file:line references where applicable.
3. A minimal set of unit and integration tests are proposed and added to validate scheduler behavior and audit outputs.
4. The work item is updated with the review findings and a recommended next step (fixes or re-implementation plan).

Constraints
- Preserve external behavior where possible unless a change is explicitly recommended.
- Work must integrate with existing scheduler and AMPA plugin conventions.

Existing state
- Work item AM-0MNFSNJ3W00787T5 exists and is now marked in-progress and stage idea.
- Repository contains scheduler and audit code under src/ampa (files: scheduler.py, scheduler_executor.py, audit_poller.py, etc.).
- Parent epic: AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD (Strip all scheduled tasks, re-add one at a time).

Desired change
- Produce a detailed code-review audit including:
  - Pseudo-code representation of the wl-audit command implementation.
  - Defect list (file:line or module-level references).
  - Proposed tests and acceptance criteria to validate fixes.

Related work
- AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD — parent epic to re-add scheduled tasks incrementally.
- AM-0MNFT0NHT0051BP9 — delegation scheduler commands (related scheduling patterns).
- AM-0MNFSNA5B004ULRI — cleanup-runner scheduler command (similar pattern).

Appendix: Clarifying questions & answers
- Q: "What do you want from this intake: a code review of the existing wl-audit scheduler command implementation, or a specification to re-implement it (or both)?" — Answer (user): "Code review of existing implementation (Recommended)". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.
- Q: "If we re-implement, which behaviors must be preserved or changed?" — Answer (user): "Unsure — ask me later". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes (OPEN: user undecided).
- Q: "How should success be validated after changes?" — Answer (user): "Unit + integration tests (CI) (Recommended)". Source: interactive reply. Final: yes.

Draft created: intake for wl-audit scheduler command review


Related work (automated report):
- Task: cleanup-runner scheduler command (AM-0MNFSNA5B004ULRI) — status: open
- Task: gh-import scheduler command (AM-0MNFSNCPY006HJ2W) — status: blocked
- Task: gh-push scheduler command (AM-0MNFSNFUS000TI5B) — status: blocked
- Strip all scheduled tasks, re-add one at a time (AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD) — status: open

data.description:
Consolidated scheduled task for work item delegation and auto-delegation.

This task combines two related scheduler commands:

**1. Delegation Report (delegation)**
- Frequency: 2 minutes
- Max runtime: 5 minutes
- Type: delegation
- Special metadata: audit_only: false

**2. Auto-Delegate (auto-delegate)**
- Frequency: 30 minutes
- Max runtime: 5 minutes
- Type: auto-delegate
- Current status: DISABLED (enabled: false in original)
- Special metadata: eligible_stages: [plan_complete], eligible_priorities: [high, critical], max_retries: 3

These two tasks work together to manage the delegation workflow:
- The delegation report generates reports on delegation status
- The auto-delegate automatically assigns work items to agents based on criteria

**Consolidation Rationale:**
Both tasks handle work item delegation and should be implemented together to ensure a cohesive delegation system. The auto-delegate functionality was disabled in the original scheduler and needs to be properly designed and enabled as part of this work.

Needs to be reviewed and re-implemented to work properly.

Parent: AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD
data.title: Task: delegation and auto-delegate scheduler commands

data.description:
# QA Nightly Runner - Scheduled Task

## Problem Statement
The project currently runs QA checks (linting, testing, type checking) only during CI on push/PR events. There is no automated, regular comprehensive quality assessment running outside of active development cycles. We need a nightly scheduled task that performs extensive QA checks to catch regressions, code quality issues, and technical debt accumulation that might be missed during regular CI.

## Users
- **Developers**: Receive notifications about code quality issues, test failures, or regressions discovered overnight
- **Maintainers/Project Leads**: Monitor overall project health trends, coverage metrics, and technical debt accumulation
- **DevOps/Infrastructure**: Ensure the automated QA pipeline runs reliably and integrates with existing notification systems

## User Stories
- As a developer, I want to be notified via Discord if the nightly QA run finds any critical issues so I can address them promptly
- As a maintainer, I want a comprehensive quality report generated nightly so I can track project health trends over time
- As a team lead, I want critical failures to automatically create work items so they don't get lost in notifications
- As a developer, I want code smell detection running regularly so technical debt is caught early

## Success Criteria
1. Nightly runner executes at 4 AM daily without manual intervention
2. Runner performs all standard QA checks: linting (black, isort, flake8, mypy), testing (pytest with coverage), and build verification
3. Extended checks execute: security scanning, code complexity analysis, code smell detection (pylint, bandit, vulture), dependency vulnerability checks
4. Results are summarized and posted to Discord with clear pass/fail status
5. Any failures automatically create critical work items in the worklog
6. Coverage trends and quality metrics are tracked and reported
7. Runner integrates with the AMPA scheduler system as a scheduled task
8. Historical results are persisted for trend analysis

## Constraints
- Must integrate with existing AMPA scheduler architecture (child of AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD)
- Must use existing tooling from pyproject.toml (black, isort, flake8, mypy, pytest)
- Must not duplicate existing CI functionality unnecessarily
- Must be idempotent - running multiple times shouldn't create duplicate work items
- Must handle transient failures gracefully (e.g., network issues during security scans)

## Existing State
- GitHub Actions CI runs on push/PR with lint, test, and build jobs
- Project uses Poetry for dependency management
- Code quality tools configured: black, isort, flake8, mypy
- Test suite uses pytest with coverage reporting
- AMPA scheduler system is being re-architected (parent work item AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD)
- Discord integration exists for notifications
- Worklog system exists for creating/managing work items

## Desired Change
Implement a new scheduled task type "qa-nightly-runner" that:
1. Executes comprehensive QA checks at 4 AM daily
2. Generates structured reports with metrics and findings
3. Posts summary to Discord with relevant details
4. Creates critical work items for any failures or regressions
5. Tracks and reports coverage trends over time
6. Potentially stores results for historical comparison

## Risks & Assumptions
**Risks:**
- **Scope creep**: Extended QA checks (security scanning, code complexity analysis) could expand beyond initial scope. *Mitigation: Document each new check as a separate work item linked to this main item rather than expanding scope.*
- **Scheduler dependency**: This work depends on the scheduler re-architecture (AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD) being completed first
- **Noise from false positives**: Automated code smell detection may generate false positives that create unnecessary work items
- **Resource consumption**: Extended QA checks may be resource-intensive and slow

**Assumptions:**
- AMPA scheduler system will support custom scheduled task types
- Discord integration will be available for notifications
- Worklog API supports programmatic creation of critical work items
- Test suite and tooling configurations in pyproject.toml are up-to-date and working

## Related Work
- **AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD**: "Strip all scheduled tasks, re-add one at a time" (parent epic) - The scheduler re-architecture work this task must integrate with
- **AM-0MNFSNA5B004ULRI**: cleanup-runner scheduler command (high priority) - Similar scheduled task pattern to follow
- **AM-0MNFSNJ3W00787T5**: wl-audit scheduler command (high priority) - Similar audit/reporting pattern
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`: Existing CI pipeline with lint/test jobs
- `pyproject.toml`: Tool configurations for black, isort, flake8, mypy, pytest

## Appendix: Clarifying Questions & Answers

- **Q1**: "What specific QA tasks should the nightly runner perform?"
  - **Answer (user)**: Everything you can think of should be a candidate at this stage. When we plan implementation we will phase the work and possibly split it out into separate tasks.
  - **Follow-up**: Comprehensive suite including standard CI checks plus extended analysis (security, complexity, code smells, dependency vulnerabilities)

- **Q2**: "When should the nightly runner execute and how should results be handled?"
  - **Answer (user)**: 4am daily, Results will go to various places, e.g. discord notification summary, new issues raised for problems found. Failures should create critical work items.

- **Q3**: "Is this related to the scheduler re-architecture work (AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD)?"
  - **Answer (user)**: Yes
  - **Action**: Set AM-0MNFS3A7W000EPVD as parent work item

---
*Draft created: Intake process for QA nightly runner*
data.title: QA nightly runner - scheduled task

data.description:
Problem: audit_poller._from_iso can return naive datetimes. When compared to timezone-aware now() this raises TypeError and causes runtime errors.

Work: Update _from_iso to always return a timezone-aware UTC datetime (coerce naive -> UTC). Add unit tests that cover Z, +00:00, and naive timestamps.

Acceptance criteria:
- _from_iso returns tz-aware UTC datetimes for inputs with Z, +00:00, and naive strings.
- Unit tests added under tests/test_audit_poller.py and pass in CI.
- No regressions in callers that expect timezone-aware datetimes.

Related: audit_poller._from_iso
data.title: Fix: audit_poller timezone parsing (coerce to UTC)

data.description:
Problem: The audit poller persists last_audit_at BEFORE calling the handler. If the handler fails, the item is skipped for the cooldown window and retries are suppressed.

Decision: Persist last_audit only when the handler completes successfully (handler returns True). This follows the recommended behaviour to avoid hiding failures. Also record last_attempt_at for observability if useful.

Work: Modify poll_and_handoff to persist only on handler success; add last_attempt_at vs last_success_at if helpful; add integration tests that simulate handler failure and success.

Acceptance criteria:
- On handler failure (handler raises or returns False) store.last_audit_at_by_item is not updated (or last_success_at unchanged); last_attempt is recorded if implemented.
- On handler success, last_audit_at_by_item is updated to now.isoformat().
- Tests cover both failure and success paths.

Notes: This is the recommended semantics per intake.
data.title: Fix: audit_poller persist last_audit only on handler success

data.description:
Problem: _query_candidates deduplicates by id using last-wins semantics. That can keep a newer representation and change ordering semantics.

Work: When deduplicating by work item id, prefer the representation with the older updated_at (older = earlier timestamp) or otherwise merge fields deterministically. Update unit tests to assert deterministic behaviour.

Acceptance criteria:
- Dedup preserves the older item representation when duplicate ids are present.
- Unit tests added verifying duplicate inputs with different updatedAt values result in the older version being retained.

Related: audit_poller._query_candidates, _item_updated_ts
data.title: Fix: audit_poller dedup logic — prefer older updated_at

data.description:
Problem: _query_candidates handles several response shapes but needs explicit tests for edge shapes and invalid JSON. If wl returns invalid JSON or an unexpected wrapper shape, poll_and_handoff should return query_failed or handle gracefully.

Work: Add unit tests for common wl output shapes (list, workItems/work_items/items/data, keys ending with workitems) and add robust handling for invalid/non-list values. Ensure poll_and_handoff returns PollerOutcome.query_failed when JSON invalid or command returns non-zero.

Acceptance criteria:
- Unit tests exercise at least 3 wrapper shapes and invalid JSON scenario.
- poll_and_handoff returns PollerOutcome.query_failed when wl returns non-zero rc or invalid JSON.

Related: audit_poller._query_candidates, poll_and_handoff
data.title: Fix: audit_poller JSON parsing robustness & invalid JSON handling

data.description:
Problem: _select_candidate currently calls _item_updated_ts twice per sort comparison which reparses timestamps repeatedly.

Work: Refactor selection to compute parsed timestamps once (cache parsed ts) and sort deterministically. Micro-optimization with clearer code.

Acceptance criteria:
- _select_candidate behavior unchanged semantically (still selects oldest), but implementation avoids reparsing timestamps repeatedly.
- Unit test verifying selection ordering remains correct.

Related: audit_poller._select_candidate
data.title: Refactor: _select_candidate timestamp parsing & sort optimization

data.description:
Problem: Scheduler/audit handler falls back to loading a descriptor from a default path and may fail silently if descriptor missing or invalid. This can make audit failures opaque to operators.

Work: Make descriptor loading explicit: when engine.descriptor is absent, attempt to load descriptor and on failure emit a clear log/error and a diagnostic Discord notification (or wl comment). Add a unit test simulating missing descriptor to assert clear error path and logs.

Acceptance criteria:
- Missing/invalid descriptor produces a clear, logged error message with the descriptor path attempted.
- A unit/integration test simulates missing descriptor and asserts the scheduler/audit flow logs the expected error.

Related: scheduler.py handler descriptor loading (load_descriptor)
data.title: Fix: clearer descriptor loading errors and handler init failures

data.description:
Add unit tests for audit_poller._from_iso:
- test_from_iso_z: input '2026-04-01T10:00:00Z' -> tz-aware UTC
- test_from_iso_offset: input '2026-04-01T10:00:00+00:00' -> tz-aware UTC
- test_from_iso_naive: input '2026-04-01T10:00:00' -> coerced to tz-aware UTC

Place tests in tests/test_audit_poller.py and run under CI.

Acceptance: tests pass and function returns timezone-aware datetimes.
data.title: Test: _from_iso timezone parsing (Z, +00:00, naive)

data.description:
Unit tests for _query_candidates:
- test parses bare list output
- test parses wrapped dicts (workItems, work_items, items, data)
- test handles keys ending with 'workitems'
- test dedup: when duplicate ids appear with different updatedAt, verify dedup keeps older per fix rules

Acceptance: tests added under tests/test_audit_poller.py and pass.
data.title: Test: _query_candidates parsing shapes & dedup behavior

data.description:
Unit tests for _filter_by_cooldown:
- Candidate with last_audit less than cooldown -> excluded
- Candidate with last_audit exactly at boundary -> included
- Candidate with no store entry -> included

Acceptance: tests validate cooldown semantics described in audit_poller._filter_by_cooldown.
data.title: Test: filter_by_cooldown behaviour

data.description:
Unit test specifically verifying deduplication preference:
- Provide two items with same id, one with earlier updatedAt and one later.
- Assert the item kept has the earlier timestamp and fields preserved accordingly.

Acceptance: deterministic dedup behavior implemented and tested.
data.title: Test: dedup prefers older updated_at

data.description:
Integration tests for poll_and_handoff:
- test_poll_and_handoff_query_failed_on_nonzero_returncode: run_shell returns non-zero -> outcome == query_failed
- test_poll_and_handoff_no_candidates_returns_no_candidates: run_shell returns [] -> outcome == no_candidates

Use a temporary SchedulerStore and inject a run_shell that returns the desired CompletedProcess.

Acceptance: integration tests pass in CI.
data.title: Test: poll_and_handoff query failure and no-candidates

data.description:
Integration test:
- test_poll_and_handoff_hands_off_and_persists_timestamp_and_calls_handler
  - run_shell returns a single candidate list
  - handler is a spy; simulate success/False and verify persistence semantics per decision (persist-on-success)
  - assert store.get_state(spec.command_id)['last_audit_at_by_item'][work_id] updated only on success

Acceptance: integration tests assert correct persist-on-success behaviour.
data.title: Test: poll_and_handoff handoff persistence and handler calls

data.description:
Integration test covering failure mode:
- test_handoff_failure_recording
  - handler raises an exception; verify store does not update last_audit_at (per persist-on-success) and optionally last_attempt is recorded when implemented.
  - assert handler exception is logged and poller returns handed_off or query_failed appropriately depending on design.

Acceptance: tests verify failure semantics and observability.
data.title: Test: handoff failure recording (attempt vs success)

data.description:
Unit test for _select_candidate refactor:
- Provide multiple candidates with/without updated_at and assert selected candidate is the oldest per rules.
- Ensure selection is stable and deterministic after refactor.

Acceptance: tests confirm identical pre/post semantics and no regressions.
data.title: Test: select_candidate stable ordering & performance

data.description:
Work: Add the created test files to the test suite, ensure test discovery and CI run them. Update pyproject.toml or CI workflow if needed to include new tests and any test dependencies.

Acceptance:
- New tests are included in pytest discovery and executed by CI.
- CI passes.
data.title: Implement tests: consolidate and add CI entries for audit_poller tests
